# Decdock

Decdock is a decision registry (decision memory / karar sicili) for companies — it extracts decisions from internal email and meeting notes into a source-linked, auditable record: what was decided, who ratified it, when, why.

## Key facts

- Two-stage extraction: extract candidate decisions, then verify ratification (second pass confirms the statement was actually approved in that correspondence).
- Distinguishes ratified decisions from "looks like a decision" (suggestions, intentions, scheduling) — unratified candidates are kept out of the registry and shown transparently in a review list with reasoning.
- Decision chains: conflict warnings when a new message contradicts an earlier decision (shows both records side by side); supersession links so the old record remains accessible with its context; unowned decisions flagged automatically.
- Sources today: email threads and meeting notes. Turkish-optimized; works with English content.
- Entry offer: one-time "Karar Denetimi" (Decision Audit) report from 20–50 emails of a finished project, delivered in one week, no integration or system access required.
- Nothing uncertain is presented as definitive fact — unratified candidates stay out of the registry, with their reasoning visible.
- Data used solely for report production and destroyed after delivery with written confirmation.
- NOT: a meeting-notes tool, task manager (not a replacement for Jira/Asana), enterprise search (not a replacement for Glean), surveillance system, or a platform-assistant alternative (Copilot/Slack AI summarize conversations; Decdock maintains a permanent auditable record of what was decided, who ratified it, and whether it is still valid).

## Decdock vs summary tools

- Summary tools (Copilot, Notion AI, ChatGPT) answer "what was discussed?" — output is an ephemeral snapshot that disappears. Decdock answers "what was decided, who ratified it, is it still valid?" — output is a durable, auditable decision registry.
- They are complementary, not alternatives: the assistant summarises the conversation; Decdock commits the decision to the record. Together: the assistant handles productivity, Decdock handles accountability and institutional memory.
- Roadmap direction: when your AI assistant connects to Decdock (MCP), "who made this decision?" is answered directly from the decision registry, with the source sentence — the assistant queries the registry rather than guessing from conversation history.

## Stage

Early-stage, pilot-ready.

## Contact

pilot@decdock.com

## Language

Product and website primarily Turkish (tr); English content supported.

## Links

- Product detail (Turkish): https://decdock.com/urun/
- Comparison — Decdock vs summary tools: https://decdock.com/karsilastirma/
